title: Clash Royale In Minecraft
description: This Is Flashz's Clash Royale In Minecraft Mod
body:
## [I Coded Clash Royale Into Minecraft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CRRpT0IopfU)

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/CRRpT0IopfU" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## How To Set Up (As A Mod):
1. Download the Clash Royale mod. The mod also requires Geckolib, Asteorbar, RemoveHud, and Essentials Mod
2. Once your game has launched with the mods, go to singleplayer world. If the Clash Royale Arena Map isnt pre installed, you can download the world from [HERE](https://www.planetminecraft.com/project/clash-royale-in-minecraft-arena/), and load it up
3. Once you've joined the world, invite your friend (using essentials) and have fun!

## Commands:
- You can do /deck gui to select your deck
- To start the game, you do /clash start
- If you need to end the game, you can /clash stop
- You can do /credit to see all the people involved and made this possible, along with all the YouTubers who voiced a character.

## Notes:
- THERE ARE BUGS!!! THIS IS EARLY ACCESS / BETA. If you find anything, it would be greatly appreciated if you let me know [in my Discord](https://discord.gg/WprgUVFwtK) in the support ticket channel, and I'll get to it straight away!
- If the world doesn't force show up in your singleplayer worlds, feel free to download the 1v1 Map [HERE](https://www.planetminecraft.com/project/clash-royale-in-minecraft-arena/)
- The owner of the world is always on the blue team

## Set it up in ONE Minute! [(Using Modpacks)](https://modrinth.com/modpack/clash-royale-in-minecraft-modpack)

<iframe width="280" height="165" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/yTXoSJUHxsU" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>
